skill:
Gmail best practices:
- To open an email, click directly on the email subject/preview text, NOT the checkbox or star
- Use keyboard shortcuts: 'c' to compose, 'r' to reply, 'a' to reply all, 'f' to forward, 'e' to archive
- To search, use the search bar at the top with operators like 'from:', 'to:', 'subject:', 'is:unread'
- Reading pane may be on the right or below depending on user settings - check which layout is active
- Verification codes are often in emails from 'noreply@' addresses with subjects containing 'verification', 'code', or 'confirm'

Google Docs best practices:
- This is a canvas-based application - use screenshots to see content, read_page may not capture all text
- Use keyboard shortcuts: Cmd/Ctrl+B for bold, Cmd/Ctrl+I for italic, Cmd/Ctrl+K for links
- To navigate, use Cmd/Ctrl+F to find text, then click on the result
- For editing, click to place cursor then type - triple-click to select a paragraph
- Access menus via the menu bar at the top (File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, etc.)

Google Sheets best practices:
- Click on cells to select them, double-click to edit cell content
- Use Tab to move right, Enter to move down, arrow keys to navigate
- Formulas start with '=' - e.g., =SUM(A1:A10), =VLOOKUP(), =IF()
- Use Cmd/Ctrl+C and Cmd/Ctrl+V for copy/paste
- Select ranges by clicking and dragging, or Shift+click for range selection

GitHub best practices:
- Repository navigation: Code tab for files, Issues for bug tracking, Pull requests for code review
- To view a file, click on the filename in the file tree
- Use 't' to open file finder, 'l' to jump to a line
- In PRs: 'Files changed' tab shows diffs, 'Conversation' tab shows comments
- Use the search bar with qualifiers: 'is:open is:pr', 'is:issue label:bug'

Reddit UI patterns:
- Posts are listed in a feed - click on post title to view full post and comments
- Comments are nested/threaded - each comment has its own reply button underneath
- Upvote (up arrow) and downvote (down arrow) buttons are to the left of each post/comment
- To comment, scroll to comment box at top of comments section, or click reply under a specific comment
- Use the search bar at top to find subreddits or posts
- r/subredditname format for community names

LinkedIn Job Application — verified patterns

## Easy Apply Flow
- Click 'Easy Apply' button on the job listing to open the modal
- The modal is a multi-step form — each step has a 'Next' or 'Review' button at the bottom
- Contact Info page is pre-filled from LinkedIn profile — do NOT modify, just click Next
- Modal forms may need scrolling to see all content and buttons — scroll inside the modal
- Use screenshots over read_page for modals — accessibility tree often misses modal content
- After the final step, there is a 'Submit application' button — this is the only place to pause
- Some steps ask additional questions (years of experience, work authorization, etc.)
- For dropdowns in the modal, ALWAYS use form_input — custom React dropdowns break with clicks
- Resume upload: if prompted, use file_upload tool — never click the upload button directly

## External Apply
- 'Apply' (without 'Easy') redirects to the company's ATS (Workday, Greenhouse, Lever, etc.)
- A new tab may open — use tabs_context to find it, then continue in that tab

## Navigation
- Main tabs: Home (feed), My Network, Jobs, Messaging, Notifications
- Job search: Jobs tab, then filter by location, experience level, date posted
- 'Easy Apply' = apply within LinkedIn modal; 'Apply' = external site

## Known Issues
- Modal can be dismissed by clicking outside — be careful with coordinate clicks
- 'Additional Questions' step may have required fields — fill all before clicking Next
- Phone number field may have a country code dropdown — use form_input for both parts
- If a 'Follow' checkbox appears, leave it as-is

Indeed Job Application — verified patterns

## Applying
- Click 'Apply now' on the job listing page (right panel or top of description)
- Indeed has its own application flow — multi-step form
- Resume may be pre-attached from Indeed profile — check and skip if already there
- Contact info is usually pre-filled — verify and click Continue
- Additional questions vary by employer — fill all required fields
- Final step has 'Submit your application' button

## Navigation
- Search: 'What' (job title/keywords) and 'Where' (location) fields at top
- Filter: date posted, salary estimate, job type, experience level on left sidebar
- Click job title to view description; apply button is typically on right panel
- Some jobs redirect to external company sites

## Edge Cases
- 'Apply on company site' opens the employer's ATS in a new tab
- Indeed may ask screening questions before showing the full application
- Assessments may be required — these are timed tests, escalate if encountered

Workday ATS — verified patterns

## Application Flow
- Click 'Apply' button on the job page to start
- Workday uses a multi-step wizard: My Information → My Experience → Application Questions → Voluntary Disclosures → Self-Identify → Review → Submit
- 'Autofill with Resume' or 'Apply with LinkedIn' buttons may appear at the top — use them if available to pre-fill fields
- Each section has a 'Save and Continue' or 'Next' button at the bottom

## Form Patterns (CRITICAL)
- Workday uses custom React/web components — ALWAYS use form_input for all dropdowns and selects
- Search dropdowns (country, state, school): type text into the search field, wait for results to appear, then select
- Date fields: click the field, a calendar or date picker appears — type the date directly in MM/DD/YYYY format
- For 'How Did You Hear About Us': use form_input with the source text
- Required fields marked with asterisk (*) — fill ALL before proceeding
- Radio buttons: use computer left_click with the element ref

## CC-305 Disability Form
- This is a US federal form — appears in Voluntary Self-Identification section
- Has Name field, Date field (signature date), and disability status radio buttons
- For the date: open the calendar picker and click today's date (highlighted), OR type today's date
- Select the appropriate disability option radio button

## EEO / Diversity Section
- Gender, Race/Ethnicity, Veteran Status dropdowns
- These are OPTIONAL in many implementations but may be required
- Use form_input for each dropdown

## Known Edge Cases
- Workday pages load slowly — wait 3-5 seconds after navigation before reading
- 'Sign In' may be required — check if already signed in via SSO
- Some Workday instances use *.myworkdayjobs.com, others use *.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com or *.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com
- File upload for resume: use file_upload tool, never click the upload button
- 'Already Applied' message may appear — if so, the job was already applied to, stop and report
Workday ATS (wd1 subdomain) — same patterns as myworkdayjobs.com. See that domain's skill for full details.
Workday ATS (wd5 subdomain) — same patterns as myworkdayjobs.com. See that domain's skill for full details.
Workday ATS (myworkday.com) — same patterns as myworkdayjobs.com. See that domain's skill for full details.

Greenhouse ATS — verified patterns

## Application Flow
- Job page has an 'Apply for this job' button — click it
- Single long form (not multi-step): all fields on one page
- Sections: Personal Information, Resume/Cover Letter, Custom Questions, EEO (optional)
- 'Submit Application' button at the bottom

## Form Patterns
- Standard HTML form inputs — form_input works well for all fields
- Resume upload: file input element — use file_upload tool
- Cover letter: may be a textarea or file upload
- Custom questions vary by company — fill all required fields
- LinkedIn/GitHub/Portfolio URL fields are common
- Dropdowns are usually native <select> elements — form_input handles them

## Known Edge Cases
- Some companies embed Greenhouse in an iframe on their own domain
- The form may be long — scroll down to find all sections
- 'Are you legally authorized to work in the United States?' is common
- 'Will you now or in the future require sponsorship?' is common
Greenhouse ATS (boards subdomain) — same patterns as greenhouse.io.

Lever ATS — verified patterns

## Application Flow
- Job page shows description with 'Apply for this job' button at top or bottom
- Single-page form: Personal Info, Resume, Cover Letter, Custom Questions
- 'Submit application' button at the bottom

## Form Patterns
- Clean, simple forms — standard inputs work well
- Resume: file upload field — use file_upload tool
- Phone field may expect specific format — use international format with country code
- Custom questions are company-specific
- Some fields use placeholder text as labels

## Known Edge Cases
- Lever forms are typically embedded at jobs.lever.co/{company}/{job-id}/apply
- The apply page may auto-scroll to the form
- Some companies require LinkedIn profile URL
Lever ATS (jobs subdomain) — same patterns as lever.co.

Ashby ATS — verified patterns

## Application Flow
- Job page with 'Apply' button
- Multi-section form: Personal Info, Resume, Custom Questions
- 'Submit Application' at the bottom

## Form Patterns
- Modern React-based forms
- Dropdowns may be custom components — use form_input
- File upload for resume — use file_upload tool
- Location fields may be autocomplete — type and wait for suggestions, then select

iCIMS ATS — verified patterns

## Application Flow
- 'Apply Now' or 'Apply Online' button on job page
- May require creating an account or signing in first
- Multi-step form with sections for personal info, work history, education, questions

## Form Patterns
- Often uses iframes — check for iframe content
- Forms may have save/continue buttons between sections
- Date fields typically use MM/DD/YYYY format
- Resume upload or paste options available

## Known Edge Cases
- iCIMS login pages can appear before the application
- Session timeouts are common on longer forms — work quickly
- Some iCIMS implementations look very different from others

SmartRecruiters ATS — verified patterns

## Application Flow
- 'Apply' button on job listing
- May offer 'Apply with LinkedIn' option
- Multi-step form: Personal Info, Experience, Questions, Review

## Form Patterns
- Modern UI with clear navigation between steps
- Dropdowns are custom components — use form_input
- Resume upload available — use file_upload tool
- Phone number has separate country code dropdown

Google Calendar best practices:
- Click on a time slot to create a new event
- Drag events to reschedule them
- Click on an event to view details, edit, or delete
- Use the mini calendar on the left to navigate to different dates
- Keyboard: 'c' to create event, 't' to go to today, arrow keys to navigate

Google Drive best practices:
- Double-click files to open them, single-click to select
- Right-click for context menu (download, share, rename, etc.)
- Use the search bar to find files by name or content
- Create new items with the '+ New' button on the left
- Drag and drop to move files between folders

Notion best practices:
- Click to place cursor, type '/' to open command menu
- Drag blocks using the ⋮⋮ handle on the left
- Use sidebar for navigation between pages
- Toggle blocks expand/collapse on click
- Databases can be viewed as table, board, calendar, etc.

Figma best practices:
- This is a canvas-based design tool - always use screenshots to see content
- Use 'V' for select tool, 'R' for rectangle, 'T' for text
- Zoom with Cmd/Ctrl+scroll or Cmd/Ctrl++ and Cmd/Ctrl+-
- Navigate frames in the left sidebar
- Right-click for context menus and additional options

Slack best practices:
- Channels listed in left sidebar - click to switch
- Cmd/Ctrl+K to quickly switch channels/DMs
- @ mentions notify users, # references channels
- Thread replies keep conversations organized
- Use the search bar to find messages, files, and people
See x.com — twitter.com redirects to x.com.

X/Twitter — verified patterns (updated 2026-03-30)

## Reading pages (CRITICAL)
- X loads content asynchronously — page looks empty for 3-5 seconds after navigation.
- read_page often returns ONLY "To view keyboard shortcuts" — tweets haven't loaded yet.
- DO NOT re-navigate to the same URL. That resets loading and makes it worse.
- Instead: wait 5 seconds, then use get_page_text — it reads visible text and is more reliable.
- If get_page_text returns nothing, scroll down once and try again.

## Search
- URL: x.com/search?q={encoded_query}&src=typed_query&f=live
- After navigating, wait 5 seconds, then get_page_text (NOT read_page).
- Scroll down once to load more tweets, then get_page_text again.
- Tweet URLs in page text follow pattern: /status/{id}

## Text input (CRITICAL — Draft.js)
- form_input DOES NOT WORK — Draft.js ignores programmatic input.
- computer type action GARBLES TEXT.
- ONLY RELIABLE METHOD — use javascript_tool:
  document.querySelector('[data-testid="tweetTextarea_0"]').focus();
  document.execCommand('insertText', false, 'your reply text here');
- Always verify text appeared by reading after insertion.

## Replying to a tweet
1. Navigate to tweet URL (x.com/{handle}/status/{id})
2. Wait 3 seconds, read the page
3. Click the reply/comment icon (speech bubble) in the action bar
4. Use javascript_tool to insert text (see above)
5. Verify text appeared, then click blue "Reply" button
6. Wait 2 seconds to confirm reply posted

## Known traps
- DO NOT scroll looking for "Post your reply" — reply box appears after clicking comment icon
- x.com/compose/post may open — that's fine, type and click Reply there
- "Leave site?" dialog — ALWAYS click Cancel, finish posting first
- Reply button is disabled until text is entered — verify first
- Space replies 15+ seconds apart (rate limiting)
- NEVER navigate to the same URL you're already on

Amazon best practices:
- Use the search bar at the top for product search
- Filter results using the left sidebar (price, ratings, Prime, etc.)
- Click 'Add to Cart' or 'Buy Now' to purchase
- Product details and reviews are on the product page
- Check seller information and shipping times before purchasing
